prompt:
Author Terraform/Terragrunt IaC and GitHub Actions to manage keyless GCP CI infrastructure for this fork (patflynn/trillian-tessera). This is for the fork's own CI on personal GCP project tessera-498520 (project number 366887815393). Produce a PR to main. DO NOT run `tofu apply`/`terragrunt apply` or mutate any cloud resources — the human applies afterward with their own credentials. Authoring + formatting + validation only.

CONTEXT — read these first:
- docs/design/gcp-ci-setup.md  (the runbook: keyless WIF design, exact gcloud resources, roles, Cloud SQL settings — translate this into IaC). Note §3 (WIF pool/provider + repo-scoped attribute condition `assertion.repository=='patflynn/trillian-tessera'`), §3d (IAM roles), §4 (Cloud SQL MYSQL_8_0, tier db-g1-small, edition enterprise, flag cloudsql_iam_authentication=on, IAM DB user = the CI SA email WITHOUT the .gserviceaccount.com suffix).
- docs/design/cloud-agnostic-storage.md (background).
- Existing Terragrunt conventions you MUST match: deployment/live/gcp/conformance/terragrunt.hcl and deployment/live/gcp/conformance/ci/terragrunt.hcl (GCS remote_state block shape, env-driven get_env(...), include "root" pattern, state bucket naming `${project}-${base}-terraform-state`), and the module layout in deployment/modules/gcp/conformance/{main,variables,outputs}.tf (provider versions / required_providers, labels, google_project_service usage). Match required_providers/version pins from the existing gcp modules. Your HCL MUST pass `terragrunt hclfmt --terragrunt-check` (see .github/workflows/terragrunt_test.yml — it checks the whole deployment/ dir).

DELIVERABLES:

1) Persistent identity stack (NEVER torn down):
   - New module deployment/modules/gcp/ci_identity/{main,variables,outputs}.tf provisioning:
     * google_project_service for: iam, iamcredentials, sts, cloudresourcemanager, storage, sqladmin, logging (APIs from runbook §2).
     * google_iam_workload_identity_pool "github-pool" + google_iam_workload_identity_pool_provider "github-provider": issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com, attribute mapping google.subject=assertion.sub, attribute.repository=assertion.repository, attribute.repository_owner=assertion.repository_owner, attribute.ref=assertion.ref, and attribute-condition `assertion.repository=='patflynn/trillian-tessera'`.
     * google_service_account "tessera-ci".
     * google_service_account_iam_member binding roles/iam.workloadIdentityUser to principalSet for attribute.repository/patflynn/trillian-tessera.
     * google_project_iam_member for the CI SA with: roles/storage.admin, roles/cloudsql.admin (NOTE: admin, not just client — the GHA must create/DELETE the SQL instance), roles/cloudsql.client, roles/cloudsql.instanceUser, roles/logging.logWriter.
     * Add `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` to the WIF pool, the provider, and the service account (these must survive any `destroy`; recreating a deleted WIF pool is blocked for 30 days).
   - Outputs: wif_provider (full resource name projects/<num>/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/github-pool/providers/github-provider), ci_sa_email, project_id, project_number, region, sql_connection_name (computed deterministically as "${project_id}:${region}:tessera-ci-mysql"), sql_iam_user (CI SA email minus the .gserviceaccount.com suffix).
   - Live config deployment/live/gcp/ci-identity/terragrunt.hcl (+ child env dir if matching the conformance parent/child pattern) with a gcs remote_state block mirroring the conformance one, env-driven via get_env GOOGLE_PROJECT / GOOGLE_REGION.

2) Ephemeral MySQL stack (this is what gets torn down nightly and recreated at will):
   - New module deployment/modules/gcp/ci_mysql/{main,variables,outputs}.tf provisioning:
     * google_sql_database_instance "tessera-ci-mysql": database_version MYSQL_8_0, tier db-g1-small, edition ENTERPRISE, region us-central1, database_flags cloudsql_iam_authentication=on, disk_size 10, disk_autoresize true. Set deletion_protection=false (it MUST be destroyable nightly).
     * google_sql_user for the CI SA: type CLOUD_IAM_SERVICE_ACCOUNT, name = CI SA email without the .gserviceaccount.com suffix.
   - Outputs: connection_name, instance_name.
   - Live config deployment/live/gcp/ci-mysql/terragrunt.hcl with its own gcs remote_state (separate state prefix from identity), env-driven. It needs the CI SA email — read via get_env (e.g. TESSERA_CI_SA_EMAIL) so the human/workflow passes the identity-stack output in.

3) Two GitHub Actions workflows (keyless, WIF/OIDC):
   - .github/workflows/ci_infra_teardown.yml: triggers `schedule` (nightly cron — pick ~09:00 UTC ≈ late evening US Pacific; add a comment noting it's UTC and easy to edit) AND `workflow_dispatch`. permissions: contents: read, id-token: write. concurrency group to avoid overlap. Steps: checkout (persist-credentials:false); google-github-actions/auth@v2 with workload_identity_provider=${{ vars.GCP_WIF_PROVIDER }}, service_account=${{ vars.GCP_CI_SA_EMAIL }}, project_id=${{ vars.GCP_PROJECT_ID }}; set up OpenTofu + Terragrunt (use the gruntwork-io/terragrunt-action@v3 pinned action already used by terragrunt_test.yml, with tofu_version/tg_version envs matching that file, OR opentofu/setup-opentofu — match the repo's existing pinned-SHA action style); run terragrunt destroy --terragrunt-non-interactive on the ci-mysql stack only, exporting the GOOGLE_PROJECT/GOOGLE_REGION/TESSERA_CI_SA_EMAIL env from repo vars.
   - .github/workflows/ci_infra_apply.yml: `workflow_dispatch` only (recreate at will — NO schedule, to avoid surprise spend). Same auth + tooling; runs terragrunt apply --terragrunt-non-interactive on the ci-mysql stack.
   - Both reference repo Variables (vars.*), never secrets: GCP_WIF_PROVIDER, GCP_CI_SA_EMAIL, GCP_PROJECT_ID, GCP_REGION. (The human sets these repo variables after applying the identity stack; you just consume them.)
   - Pin third-party actions by commit SHA like the existing workflows do (see terragrunt_test.yml / go_test.yml).

4) deployment/live/gcp/ci-identity (and ci-mysql) README.md files, plus a short top-level note in deployment/README.md (or a new docs section) explaining: the persistent vs ephemeral split, why WIF must never be destroyed (30-day name reservation), the env vars each stack reads, the bootstrap order (apply identity once locally as Owner -> set the 4 repo variables from its outputs -> apply mysql), and how teardown/recreate works (the two workflows + local terragrunt).

CONSTRAINTS / ACCEPTANCE:
- Match existing deployment/ conventions exactly (provider versions, remote_state shape, hcl style). `terragrunt hclfmt --terragrunt-check --terragrunt-diff` over deployment/ MUST pass. Run `tofu fmt -recursive deployment/` and `terragrunt hclfmt` before committing.
- Run `tofu validate` on each module using `-backend=false` (no cloud creds needed; do NOT `terragrunt apply`/`tofu apply`/`tofu init` against the real GCS backend). If validate needs init, use `tofu init -backend=false`.
- Use OpenTofu (`tofu`) — terraform is unfree in nixpkgs. A devShell exists: `nix develop -c tofu ...` / `nix develop -c terragrunt ...` provide the tools (flake.nix at repo root). 
- Do NOT add branch protection, the GCS+MySQL conformance test workflow, or any apply/bootstrap automation — those are separate follow-ups. Scope = the two IaC stacks + the teardown/apply workflows + docs.
- Do NOT commit any real secrets or long-lived keys (the whole point is keyless).
- Run the repo's relevant checks before opening the PR; ensure no existing Go/test/lint targets are broken by your additions (you're only adding deployment/ + .github/workflows/ + docs, so keep the blast radius there).

PR title: "Add OpenTofu/Terragrunt CI infra (keyless WIF + ephemeral Cloud SQL) with nightly GHA teardown". In the PR body, summarize the stacks, the bootstrap order, and explicitly note the human still needs to (a) apply the identity stack as Owner, (b) set the 4 repo variables, (c) run the apply workflow / local apply for mysql.
